decisions: ACCEPT M1-GATE for lead_orchestrator integration. The M1 acceptance matrix is satisfied by the closed SIM-001 through SIM-006 evidence, systems gate doc, local validation record, and CI evidence.

The missing literal real Chrome Dedicated Worker plus packaged Electron host hash extraction is acceptable as non-blocking for M1 only because docs/GATE-M1.md explicitly does not claim that proof, classifies the gap as PASS_WITH_INTERPRETATION plus FOLLOW_UP_REQUIRED, and current CI validates browser and desktop smoke while authority logic remains in shared simulation code.

No hidden R4 decision, frozen product/platform/core architecture change, security model change, save/schema expansion beyond accepted M1 Save Envelope v1, production dependency, or M2 implementation start was found in the reviewed gate scope.
The current coordination-state last_main_commit drift is non-product state and does not block the technical gate, but lead_orchestrator must reconcile it during gate integration.
risks: FOLLOW_UP_REQUIRED: add an explicit Chromium plus Electron host hash canary before M2 exit or earlier if platform-host determinism is claimed.
FOLLOW_UP_REQUIRED: add a sim-core/content-runtime boot-boundary contract test when M2 begins real world/content integration.
FOLLOW_UP_REQUIRED: reconcile project/goal-mode-state.json last_main_commit from 021c2545438af71455547abf9761cf671f39c280 to the integrated gate/main commit during lead integration.
Known non-blocking MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning remains in the Worker fixture test path.
requested_action: lead_orchestrator: integrate the accepted M1-GATE evidence, keep the Chrome/Electron host-hash and content-runtime boot-boundary items as explicit follow-up labels/risks, reconcile coordination-state drift, and do not start M2 implementation until normal lead integration gates pass.
